title: A day in the life of the last lighthouse keeper
summary: Describe the daily routine of an elderly lighthouse keeper before the light was automated.
topic: The daily routine of an elderly lighthouse keeper
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences describing someone's daily routine in the past. Say what they did each day, in what order, and how they felt about their work. Use past simple and time expressions (in the morning, at noon, before dark, at night).
hintStarters: Every morning at five o'clock, the keeper climbed the stairs and…
He spent most of the afternoon watching the sea because…
After many years alone in the lighthouse, he said that…

hintVocab.meaningVi: phòng đèn trên đỉnh hải đăng
còi sương, còi báo sương mù
đang trực, đang làm nhiệm vụ
vụ đắm tàu, xác tàu đắm

sampleText: Before lighthouses were automated, a keeper named Einar Solberg lived and worked alone on a small island off the coast of Norway for twenty-two years. Every morning at five o'clock, he climbed the spiral stairs to the lantern room and checked that the great lens was clean. He wiped it carefully with a soft cloth. He then trimmed the wick and added fuel so the light was ready for that night. In the morning, he also wrote in the logbook: the weather, the wind direction, and any ships he saw on the horizon. He ate breakfast alone and listened to the radio. In the afternoon, he walked around the island to check for damage after storms. Before dark, he climbed the stairs again and lit the light. The beam began to turn, and it sent out a long flash every fifteen seconds. At night, he stayed near the lamp and listened. In fog, he also activated the foghorn, a deep sound that carried far across the water. He rarely slept more than a few hours. He said later that the hardest part was not the isolation but the responsibility. He knew that ships depended on that light. When the lighthouse was finally automated in 1987, Einar packed his few belongings into two bags and took the supply boat to the mainland. He said he felt proud but also a little unnecessary.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Time expressions to structure a past routine
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả thói quen hàng ngày trong quá khứ, dùng các cụm thời gian như 'every morning', 'in the morning', 'in the afternoon', 'before dark', 'at night' để cho biết khi nào từng việc xảy ra. Những cụm này thường đứng đầu câu. Ví dụ: 'Every morning at five o'clock, he climbed the stairs', 'In the afternoon, he walked around the island', 'Before dark, he climbed the stairs again'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When describing a past daily routine, use time expressions such as 'every morning', 'in the morning', 'in the afternoon', 'before dark', 'at night' to show when each thing happened. These phrases often come at the start of a sentence. Examples: 'Every morning at five o'clock, he climbed the stairs', 'In the afternoon, he walked around the island', 'Before dark, he climbed the stairs again'.

sampleTranslationVi: Trước khi các hải đăng được tự động hóa, một người gác tên Einar Solberg sống và làm việc một mình trên một hòn đảo nhỏ ngoài khơi bờ biển Na Uy suốt hai mươi hai năm. Mỗi buổi sáng lúc năm giờ, ông leo lên cầu thang xoắn ốc đến phòng đèn và kiểm tra xem thấu kính lớn đã sạch chưa. Ông lau thận trọng bằng miếng vải mềm. Sau đó ông tỉa bấc đèn và thêm nhiên liệu để đèn sẵn sàng cho đêm đó. Vào buổi sáng, ông cũng ghi vào nhật ký: thời tiết, hướng gió và những con tàu nào ông thấy trên đường chân trời. Ông ăn sáng một mình và nghe radio. Vào buổi chiều, ông đi bộ quanh đảo để kiểm tra thiệt hại sau bão. Trước khi trời tối, ông lại leo cầu thang và thắp đèn. Chùm sáng bắt đầu quay và phát ra một tia sáng dài cứ mỗi mười lăm giây. Ban đêm, ông ở lại gần ngọn đèn và lắng nghe. Trong sương mù, ông cũng bật còi sương — một âm thanh trầm vang xa trên mặt nước. Ông hiếm khi ngủ nhiều hơn vài tiếng. Sau này ông nói rằng phần khó nhất không phải là sự cô đơn mà là trách nhiệm. Ông biết rằng các con tàu phụ thuộc vào ánh đèn đó. Khi hải đăng cuối cùng được tự động hóa vào năm 1987, Einar đóng gói ít đồ đạc của mình vào hai chiếc túi và đi thuyền tiếp tế về đất liền. Ông nói ông cảm thấy tự hào nhưng cũng hơi thừa thãi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that best begins a description of a past daily routine.
options: Every morning at five o'clock, he climbs the stairs.
Every morning at five o'clock, he is climbing the stairs.
Every morning at five o'clock, he was climbed the stairs.
Every morning at five o'clock, he climbed the stairs.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past habitual actions use past simple ('climbed'). 'Climbs' is present; 'is climbing' is present continuous; 'was climbed' is wrong (no passive meaning here).
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense describes a regular habit in the past?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'so that' to show purpose?
options: He added fuel so the light would be ready for that night.
He added fuel so the light was ready for that night.
He added fuel so that the light is ready for that night.
He added fuel for the light to ready for that night.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'so that' in a past context, 'would' is the correct modal for expressing purpose. Option A is also acceptable informally, but 'would be' is the standard reported purpose form. Option D misses the verb 'be'.
mcQuestions.hint: In a past context, which modal follows 'so that' for purpose?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly says the keeper rarely slept.
options: He slept rarely more than a few hours.
He rarely slept more than a few hours.
He rarely slept more than few hours.
He rarely sleep more than a few hours.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Rarely' goes before the main verb. 'A few' requires the article 'a'; 'few' alone means 'almost none'. 'Sleep' is present simple, not past.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'rarely' go? Does 'few hours' need the article 'a'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly contrasts what was hard with what was not hard?
options: The hardest part was not the isolation, but the responsibility.
The hardest part was neither the isolation or the responsibility.
The hardest part was not the isolation but the responsibility.
The hardest part was not isolation but responsibility.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Not … but …' is the correct contrastive structure without a comma (for closely paired contrasts). 'Neither … or' is wrong; the correct pair is 'neither … nor'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which contrastive structure correctly pairs 'not X but Y'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that says ships relied on the lighthouse.
options: Ships depended of that light.
Ships depended from that light.
Ships depending on that light.
Ships depended on that light.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Depend on' is the correct collocation. 'Depending on' is a participle; without a helping verb it cannot be the main verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition follows 'depend' as a verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'packed … into'.
options: He packed his few belongings into two bags.
He packed his few belongings in two bags.
He packed his few belongings to two bags.
He packed his few belongings two bags.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Pack something into a container' means to put things inside by pressing them in. 'Into' implies movement inside the bags.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition shows movement into a container when packing?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means the daily written record that a lighthouse keeper kept?
options: lens
log
beam
wick
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'log' (or logbook) is an official daily record of events, especially on a ship or lighthouse. A 'lens' focuses light; a 'beam' is a ray of light; a 'wick' is the part of a lamp that burns.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means a written daily record of events?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence about the lighthouse's automation that correctly uses 'when'.
options: When the lighthouse automated, he packed his bags.
When the lighthouse was automated, he has packed his bags.
When the lighthouse was automated, he packed his bags.
When the lighthouse will be automated, he packed his bags.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both clauses use past simple: 'was automated' (passive) and 'packed'. 'Will be' in a past story is wrong; 'has packed' is present perfect, not past simple.
mcQuestions.hint: When both events are in the past, what tense goes in both clauses after 'when'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that uses 'felt' correctly with two contrasting feelings.
options: He felt proud but also a little unnecessarily.
He felt proudly but also a little unnecessary.
He felt proud but also a bit unnecessarily.
He felt proud but also a little unnecessary.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'felt', use adjectives ('proud', 'unnecessary'), not adverbs ('proudly', 'unnecessarily'). 'A little' modifies an adjective; 'a bit' works the same way but would need 'unnecessary' (adjective), not an adverb.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'felt', do you use an adjective or an adverb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly describes the light's pattern.
options: The beam sent out a long flash every fifteen seconds.
The beam sent out a long flash each fifteen seconds.
The beam sent out a long flash every fifteen second.
The beam sent out a long flash all fifteen seconds.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Every + number + plural noun' is the correct frequency structure: 'every fifteen seconds'. 'Each fifteen seconds' is not standard for intervals. 'All fifteen seconds' does not mean the same thing.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word, 'every' or 'each', is used for repeating intervals of time?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes someone doing a quiet, isolated job for many years?
options: He lived and worked alone in a small island for twenty-two years.
He lived and worked alone on a small island for twenty-two years.
He lived and worked alone on a small island since twenty-two years.
He lived and worked alone on a small island during twenty-two years.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'On an island' is the correct preposition (islands are surfaces). 'For + period' is used with past simple for a completed duration. 'Since' requires a starting point (a year or event), not a period. 'During' needs a named period.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition is used for location on an island? Which time expression means 'a completed duration'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Einar felt proud when the lighthouse was automated, but also 'a little unnecessary'. Have you ever finished something important and felt both happy it was done and strange that it was over? What made it feel that way?
